title: human-mem Episode Spec v0
description: The intermediate artifact between the expensive AI extraction step and the cheap render/personalization step (decision D-009). One spec per narrative unit (PR, session, or incident). Renderers (browser viewer, VS Code webview, GitHub comment) consume this; the learner model sizes panels at assembly time.

source.description: Provenance. Everything in the episode must be traceable here (no fabricated facts).

narrative_density.description: Grading per plan.md; decides which renderer template applies (open question Q-4 owns the classifier).

title.description: Episode title, story-like, no clickbait lies.

logline.description: One-sentence hook shown in the feed.

concepts.description: Concepts this episode exercises. Renderer joins these against the learner-model exposure ledger to decide intro-panel depth (D-005). ids are lowercase slugs shared across episodes.

intro.description: Pre-training material (Mayer). Two depths; renderer picks by exposure.

full.description: Character-intro panel text for first-timers. Analogy-first.

area.description: Codebase area this concept lives in (learner-model unit Q-2, both axes recorded).

cast.description: People/agents as characters. Only include when they carry causal structure (seductive-details rule, goal.md).

panels.description: Ordered story beats. MUST follow the fixed grammar (D-006): incident -> hypotheses -> prediction -> reveal -> resolution -> teaser. Renderer maps beat kinds to visual templates.

beat.description: concept-intro panels are PLACEHOLDERS: renderer expands/collapses them per learner model. prediction panels MUST precede their reveal panel and be tap-gated in HTML (D-004).

speaker.description: cast id, for speech-bubble attribution. Omit for narration.

concept_ref.description: For concept-intro beats: which concepts[].id to expand here.

hypothesis_id.description: For hypothesis beats: stable id referenced by prediction options and reveal.

body.description: Panel text. Markdown-lite (bold, code spans). Everything factual must trace to source.

figure.description: Optional intuition figure (dual coding). You emit STRUCTURED DATA ONLY — the renderer draws the SVG; never write SVG yourself. Pick the type matching the causal shape. Put figures on NARRATION panels (no speaker).

edges.description: flow/architecture: directed edges; kind:'fault' is the wrong path (red/dashed).

steps.description: sequence: ordered {label,fault?} events. scene: ordered animation beats (see actors).

fault.description: this is the breaking step / the moment it goes wrong

actor.description: scene: subject actor id (source for move/flow)

to.description: scene: target actor id for move/flow

text.description: scene: token text for type/move (e.g. the message)

note.description: scene: one-line caption shown while this beat plays

actors.description: scene: positioned boxes on a 0..100 x 0..100 stage. x/y are the box CENTER in stage units.

code.description: Optional code exhibit for code-walk beats.

caption.description: The literate-diff sentence: what this change means in the story.

prediction.description: For prediction beats: the retrieval-forcing interaction. Fair-play rule: for beginner questions all clues must be in prior panels.

answer.description: option id. 'both'/'neither' style answers allowed as explicit options.

feedback.description: Per-option feedback. Hypercorrection: wrong-answer feedback explains WHY the intuition fails, kindly.

decisions.description: Design decisions surfaced for ratification (core-loop step 3). What was chosen, what was discarded, the trade-off.

boundaries.description: Boundaries & assumptions (situation-model element 3): where this change stops working.

quiz.description: End-of-episode quiz, 3-5 items, medium difficulty, decision-mimicking (transfer-appropriate processing). Same option/feedback shape as prediction.

map_updates.description: What this episode adds to the accreting codebase map (series bible). Episode=why, map=shape.

note.description: One line: what now exists there and why.

teaser.description: Next-episode hook. Honest (points at a real open thread in the repo).
